plantPot.hover_text: Variable inspector: plantPot. Environmental property hints under this asset layer can be revealed using targeted methods.

lockedDrawer.hover_text: Variable inspector: lockedDrawer. A locked container object requiring an unquoted key variable parameter reference to unlock.

baker_sheep.hover_text: Entity reference: Baker Sheep. A frantic character entity waiting to pass an active quest string to your console tracker.

win.title: Case Accepted!
win.subtitle: The Bakery Break-in
win.nextToast: 🥖 Following the Sheep to the Bakery!
win.completeDialogue: The Baker Sheep pulls your sleeve urgently. 'Please! Follow me to the shop floor before the tracks go cold!' You gather your notebook and step out into the street.
win.completeHint: Accompany the Baker to the crime scene.

phases.comment: ACTION 1: Global room search using a string parameter.
phases.speaker: Bunny's Notebook
phases.dialogue: [INSTRUCTION MANUAL LOG]: To inspect an unmapped room environment layout, look around by calling the search method directly on your identity object using the specific instruction string: **self.search("look around room");**
phases.output:
Scanning room configuration...
→ Environment items mapped to workspace notebook panel.
→ Active structural links discovered: plantPot, lockedDrawer.
phases.lesson: Welcome to the terminal! To start an action, command your character object using self followed by an action method. A single string argument passed inside quotes tells your lizard identity exactly what behavior to manifest.
lizard_notes: OBJECT VARIABLE: plantPot (The office vegetation anchor)
OBJECT VARIABLE: lockedDrawer (Bunny's secret desk partition)

phases.comment: ACTION 2: Targeted multi-parameter search mapping positioning strings against variables.
phases.speaker: Bunny's Notebook
phases.dialogue: [FIELD NOTES ARCHIVE]: When searching specific spatial locations, pass a description string parameter alongside the target object variable. Commas separate parameters, and case sensitivity matters. To scan beneath the office foliage, type: **self.search("under", plantPot);**
phases.output:
Moving target assets...
→ You peer directly under the base of the plantPot.
→ Found: [🔑 key] left behind in the tray indentation!
phases.lesson: Functions can receive multiple parameters separated by commas. Notice that "under" is a string layout description, while plantPot is an unquoted object variable anchor existing in the room.
lizard_notes: ITEM EVIDENCE: key (Discovered beneath plantPot foliage)

phases.comment: ACTION 3: Execute method passing two unquoted live object variable parameters.
phases.speaker: Lizard Instincts
phases.dialogue: Alright, we successfully recovered the key object from the foliage. Now we need to try applying it to the cabinet variable. Let's execute our standard multi-parameter activation method to unlock the drawer asset: **self.unlock(lockedDrawer, key);**
phases.output:
Click! The lock mechanisms tumble smoothly across the channel interface.
→ Vault state: OPEN.
phases.lesson: By matching the raw unquoted variable identities (lockedDrawer and key) inside your method arguments, the environment updates its structural tracking parameters instantly.

phases.comment: TRANSITION CUTSCENE: Baker character assumes the speaker track.
phases.speaker: Baker Sheep
phases.dialogue: Help! Help! 🥖 Someone broke into my bakery! Detective, please, everything is in total ruins and I absolutely need your expert assistance right away!
phases.output:
WARNING: I am not a detective
→ Baker Sheep ignores the terminal print statement completely!
phases.lesson: console.warn() prints a highlighted warning array to the data stream. Perfect for flashing urgent system disclaimers!
lizard_notes: CRIME LOG: Sudden Bakery Break-in reported.
URGENT TASK: Follow the Baker Sheep.
